job_description:
# Healthcare Data Analyst
Company: Kootenai Health
Source: Indeed
Employment type: Full-time
Checked: April 27, 2026
URL: https://www.indeed.com/viewjob?jk=301617d4d726e2e5

- Remote healthcare data analyst role.
- Supports quality, safety, utilization, cost, and risk reporting from multiple data sources.

replacements.replace: Healthcare Data Analyst | SQL | Utilization & Quality Reporting | Data Validation
replacements.find: ETL Analyst | Healthcare Data Analyst | Data Quality & Warehouse Analyst

replacements.replace: Healthcare Data & ETL Analyst with 7+ years of experience validating, reconciling, and delivering trusted healthcare data for reporting and operational analysis. Deep expertise in advanced SQL, data quality, ETL validation, and production data support, with strong relevance to utilization, cost, quality, and risk-focused reporting.
replacements.find: Healthcare Data & ETL Analyst with 7+ years of experience validating, reconciling, and delivering trusted enterprise data across healthcare (payer), banking, retail, and e-commerce domains. Deep hands-on expertise in advanced SQL, ETL validation, source-to-target testing, data quality, data warehousing, and production data support. Currently extending core skills into Snowflake, Databricks, Azure Data Factory, and Python to support cloud modernization and AI-ready data pipelines. Proven partner to engineering, QA, and business stakeholders—translating mapping documents and business rules into validation frameworks that protect reporting accuracy, regulatory compliance, and downstream analytics readiness.

replacements.replace: Contributed to data quality and reporting accuracy initiatives that reduced post-release data hotfixes by ~25% and improved stakeholder trust in dashboards, utilization metrics, and downstream KPIs.
replacements.find: Contributed to data quality and reporting accuracy initiatives that reduced post-release data hotfixes by ~25% and improved stakeholder trust in payer-operations dashboards and downstream KPIs.

gaps: Production management reporting framing is broader
notes: Tailored toward utilization, cost, quality reporting, and production-ready data support.
